title: Han/CJK via KanjiVG (CC-BY-SA)
context: Han/CJK characters are used in Chinese, Japanese, and Korean writing and have established stroke orders (documented in KanjiVG, CC-BY-SA). Using KanjiVG as a source triggers share-alike: any derived model layer built from it inherits CC-BY-SA and must be kept separable from CC-BY-only models. This task is blocked on a policy decision (adopt CC-BY-SA models in-repo or keep strictly CC-BY and defer Han scripts) and on securing a script-literate CJK reviewer.
objective: Derive Han/CJK glyph stroke-order models from KanjiVG (CC-BY-SA), commit them under CC-BY-SA-4.0 as a separable model layer, with grid-based guideline geometry and script-literate reviewer sign-off — conditional on the CC-BY-SA policy decision and reviewer availability.
acceptanceCriteria: BLOCKER: CC-BY-SA policy decision confirmed by the maintainer (adopt SA models in-repo as a separable layer, or defer Han scripts entirely).
BLOCKER: A script-literate CJK reviewer (qualified teacher or native/fluent writer of a Han-script language) is named and confirmed.
Han/CJK glyph models derived from KanjiVG: stroke paths, stroke order, and start points extracted and expressed as schema-valid GlyphModel JSON with sourceRef to KanjiVG.
KanjiVG CC-BY-SA license honored: derived model layer committed under CC-BY-SA-4.0 and kept separable from CC-BY-only models in the repo structure.
Grid guideline system (square cell per character) defined in GuidelineSystem.
Gate-verified open CJK font sourced (gate-002 PASS artifact); SOURCES.md committed with full provenance and KanjiVG snapshot.
Script-literate reviewer signs the per-glyph checklist.
resources: PLAN.md section 7C (share-alike handling — KanjiVG CC-BY-SA), section 16 Q5 (SA policy open question)
output: data/han/ directory (separable CC-BY-SA layer) with schema-valid GlyphModel JSON files derived from KanjiVG, SOURCES.md with KanjiVG provenance, reviewer-signed checklist, and trace-sheet PDFs — committed CC-BY-SA-4.0.
